You notice the cloud-choked sky overhead growing a shade darker than usual and you retreat to a covered alleyway just as the first drops of the planet’s acid rain begins to fall. A few others have taken shelter in the alley with you, including a few rushers, like yourself. The galactic travelers stick out like a sore thumb on this planet, so the natives crowd together at the far end of the alley. Because it’s just you and the other off-world aliens, you can easily overhear their conversation despite the steady, sizzling shower nearby. “You’re telling me they’ll pay you just for taking some new mod?” one man says. Judging by his kaithrit tails, kui-tan ears, and gryvain wings, he’s no stranger to gene modding. His counterpart, a brightly colored ovir, shakes her head. “Nah, it’s not just that. It’s some local mod. Real experimental stuff. Trying to compete with Xenogen, maybe? That shit’s not safe.” “Oh, what’s the worst that could happen?” the first rusher asks. The two fall silent and stare into the acid rain storm for a few minutes. “Okay, fair enough,” the chimeric man admits. “But I’d like to at least check it out myself. Where are they doing it?” “Some Peer at the Arovok milk bar, supposedly. But credits or no: you’d never catch me playing lab rat for these shady-as-fuck zaibatsu Peers.” The rain gradually recedes and you’re free to continue with what you were doing. textify(OM||(OM=(0,Bt.Z)(["Although you don’t know much about this ‘Dame Xhanni,’ you suppose it’s probably about time you learned a bit about how the other half lives. And the elegant native’s offer was fairly intriguing, so you move towards the steward she left behind. The residents of the Gyre give both the cyber soldier and her bike a wide berth, apparently wanting nothing to do with the mechanized private guard - and you can hardly blame them, after your first tussle. But, when you approach the valet, the silver-chromed XO snaps to crisp attention and bows parallel to the ground in a deep gesture of deference. “Your invitation has been registered,” the armored drone intones in an affectless voice. “Please accompany me to-” she stops speaking abruptly, jerking upright and spinning on the tips of her toes to gesture at the huge, heavily armored swoop bike she’s been parked next to before continuing, “your ride.” You follow the cyborg to the hover bike, a low whistle on your lips. You’re vaguely aware that these kinds of vehicles have fallen out of favor among inner planetary circles, but this is some serious hardware! The engines alone must be worth a fortune to the right bike-nut, and the flanks are decked out in segmented armor plates that give the impression they help the damn thing go faster, rather than slow it down with extra weight. It’s even been fitted with a shield generator of its own, the flickering purple crackle cascading across its surface until the XO is a step away and deactivates it with a twitch of her head. The silver soldier swings a thickly-muscled leg up, her foot tracing a long circle up over her head before coming down on the far side of the bike, mounting with a gymnast’s flourish. She plugs her huge, cyber arm into the console and wraps her organic limb around the handlebars before turning back to you. “Please take your seat and hold onto me tightly. This is a high velocity trip.”"])))

It’s a good thing the XO’s got such a heavy-duty craft - anything smaller would never fit your considerable, tauric bulk. You trot over and take a banking bounce, leaping onto the vehicle without much trouble. It does sink slightly under your weight, but the swoop stabilizes after a few seconds. You flex your [pc.legOrLegs] to get a firm grip on the bike, but go ahead and wrap your arms around the augmented woman’s muscular midsection as well.

You certainly don’t need to be told twice to hold on tightly. Slithering up onto the heavy-duty craft, you send your [pc.legOrLegs] out to wrap around the XO’s waist and abdomen. Binding coils of your naga body tighten against the augmented women’s muscular midsection all the way up to her breastplate, and you thread your arms under hers to add an extra tight hug to the secure embrace.

The XO adjusts the hover distance of the heavy-duty craft to accommodate your height, allowing you to mount easily. Swinging your [pc.legOrLegs] around the extended seat, you scoot up close to the augmented woman. Wrapping your arms around her muscular midsection, you hug tight and brace yourself against her iron-stiff spine. You can’t help but enjoy the feeling of her ample rump, squished up against your loins, but elect to keep that to yourself.

textify(HM||(HM=(0,Bt.Z)(["Once you’ve settled into place, the combat doll twitches her forearm and the aerial chopper is off. The vehicle is as fleet as it looks, zooming through the towering pinnacles of Dhaal at such speed that the glittering city is a neon blur. The crowded streets of the Gyre diminish rapidly, though it’s hard to see anything clearly with the wind whipping at your face. From your elevation, you can see that the private, sky-top sanctum of the Peers is roughly laid out in a triangle, brilliant blues, reds, and greens edging elegant artifice of breath-taking beauty. From up here, it’s almost easy to forget that the people down below live amid acid rain and toxic smog. The swoop heads into the [favoredFactionCorp] side of the skyscrapers, banking and rising until it reaches the penthouses. Acceleration drains from the two of you as your chauffeur lands on a wide runway porch with perfect precision, planting her foot as the bike comes to a stop. “We have arrived. You may let go of me at your convenience.” The XO tilts her head ninety degrees, though you assume that’s just for your benefit, since her chrome helmet completely conceals the upper half of her face, eyes and all. You slowly disentangle yourself from the roboticized girl, though not without a tinge of regret at having to release your hold on those ripped abs. You dismount and your driver unplugs her heavy cyber-arm from the vessel. With careless ease, she lifts her legs out sidewise in a perfect split and - bracing herself on the seat with one arm - executes a sitting backflip off the bike and onto the balls of her feet in front of you. “Please, follow me to the boudoir.” For anybody else, the fancy footwork or the shameless invitation might be cause for remark, but the cyborg’s emotionless tone robs both of their heart-fluttering effects. She’s been programmed this way and that’s all there is. She’s not showing off or suggesting any romantic entanglements. At least, not with her."])))
You bite your lip and glance back at the bike. If you put your face on the seat, maybe she’d do that little move backwards for you...
You shake your head. Every time you think there’s something beautiful about Dhaal, you’re reminded that the whole thing is built on those above victimizing those below.
What a waste! Any girl who can do splits like that is somebody you’d LOVE to get to know better.
You scowl at the girl’s impassive half-face. Boy, these zaika can’t help but rub their toys in your face at every turn, can they?
With sigh, you follow the supple cyborg’s frame into your host’s personal sanctum, ready to start this ‘private party.’

The penthouse is a marvelous affair, even by Peer standards. Dark, glistening tile sweeps across the floor, while sumptuously thick carpets line the stairs that lead to an overlooking second level. The center of the ground floor is filled with a crystal clear pool that shimmers with the rising steam of its heated waters. A number of bioluminescent, alien plants and exotic-yet-tasteful art pieces line the perimeter, flanking decadently plush seats that look practically as huge as beds and covered in the draped folds of some kind of gold-threaded silken sheets. The second level is no less furnished, though more modern than elegant. Expansive shelves hold rows upon rows of flickering tablets, neon-lit screens, and a well-supplied bar.
A door on the upper level opens and your host gracefully makes her entrance with a delighted nod in your direction.

The woman has a darker purple skin tone, smooth curves nearly flawless but for the vertical seam of glowing blue cybernetics on the right side of her face and a single, white freckle by her left eye. Her natural eye color is pink, her cybernetic eye, blue; both cast the vibrant glowing light of her kind’s luminescent eyeshine of her people. She wears her pale tresses long, the sweeping curtain of alabaster swishing past her hips with each movement.

The woman has a healthy, purple skin tone, smooth curves nearly flawless but for the seams and neon lights of a cybernetic augment at pelvis level. She has bright, crimson eyes that glow with the luminance of her kind, and a pair of thin, pink shades she wears on the tip of her nose. Her expansively long hair is done up in coiled buns at neck level, and held in place with a pair of long hairpin sticks.

The woman has bright purple skin, her smooth curves nearly flawless but for the freckles on her cheeks and the seam of a cybernetic augment just under the bulb of her tail. She has big, green eyes that glow with the luminance of her kind, and wears a pair of exceedingly large, oval glasses, firmly pushed up to the brim of her nose.

The peer wears an intricate torq around her neck tight enough to be a collar, along with gold, dangling earrings and an artfully decorated tail ring under her modestly-covered bulb. In all, she stands as a 7' tall sculpture of zaika perfection.

“What a wonderful treat: you’ve graced me with some company for the endless evening. As I mentioned, I’m called Dame Xhanni, but I insist you simply call me Anni. I trust your trip was pleasant enough? My girl there is quite keen on riding her big swoop bike as fast as she can, and it can be a... breath-taking experience at times.” The other woman doesn’t react, but judging by the trip you just took, you’d guess she’s right on the money.

“Typically XOs are given numbers, but I couldn’t keep myself from naming her. Oh Hollow, please take a bow.” No sooner is the command issued, but your silver-encased driver is bent so low her head nearly touches the ground.

Dame Xhanni looks as elegantly presentable as ever, though you can see right past the airs of formality to the over-sexed slut below. “[pc.name] Steele, I am ever so pleased to see you again. Many of my sisters in [favoredFactionCorp] have been murmuring about our dalliances, and I’m ever so eager to give them more fuel for their rumors!”

She slowly descends the stairs, her sharp heels clicking even through the carpeting. “Please, join me for a dip in the pool as we whisper of things that are and things that might be.” As she comes closer, the executive carelessly sheds her fashionable finery, one article of gossamer silk after another on each step, until she’s bare of everything but her tail sock and standing inches away from you. Anni runs her fingertips across your cheek as she sweeps past in a cloud of perfume that leaves your body tingling. Once she reaches the edge of the pool, she steps out of her high heels and dips a two-toed foot into the water before glancing back with a come-hither look.

A shiver runs down your body as you remember the effects of her ‘pool water’ from last time. You pay careful attention to the zaika Peer and think you catch the slight dilation of her eyes as she dips her toes in. You’re a little nervous about losing yourself like before, but... After all, why would you have accepted her invitation if you weren’t more than a little tempted?

Something tells you that Dame Xhanni isn’t one to change a winning formula, so the water is likely just as aphro-laced as it was on your first meeting. The thought of losing yourself to the depths of lustful indulgence is a pretty damn tempting one. Besides, she’s already dosed herself, and what kind of guest would you be, if you left the girl in such a dire state?

You know damn well what’s in that pool and - to be honest - it takes all your self control not to just jump in, gear and all. You can’t wait to feel the aphrodisiac-laced waters seep into your every pore. To descend into the heart-pounding sexual frenzy of a hunger that grows sharper with every meal. To lead a roaring procession of throbbing passion all over the Peer’s penthouse. It’s why you accepted the invitation, after all.

You lose your [pc.gear] with haste, joining Anni in the pool once you’ve stripped down to your [pc.skinFurScales]. The water is invitingly warm without being unbearably hot, and it’s all you can do not to sigh in blissful delight once you’ve submerged yourself in the lightly bubbling spa. Your hostess takes a seat next to you on a bench at the side of the pool, her restless E-cup tits nearly so covered in the waves you could almost mistake them for the ever-full milkers of the planet’s immigrant cowgirl population.\n\n“I am bound to the caprices of my guest’s delights,” she coos, running her fingers over your shoulder and leaning into you with lidded eyes. “Whatever shall we discuss?”

"+textify(MM||(MM=(0,Bt.Z)(["Xhanni politely covers a laugh with one hand while she swishes her tail, keeping the bulb cover above the water. “The peerage, is it? Yes, I understand things must look quite strange to outsiders. Richer, healthier... even taller than those down below.” She sighs and shakes her head. With a gesture, she beckons her XO to come to the poolside. The cyborg does as she’s bid and gets down to her knees so her Mistress can stroke her cheek, the zaika’s three-fingered hand lingering wistfully. “We are well off, it is true. Our desires are met, our fancies indulged, and our ambitions rattle the pillars of the city. We can buy whatever - and whoever - we so choose. But it would be a mistake to assume that we are any safer than those on the streets, despite our station.” “Corporate espionage?” you ask. “Certainly,” she confirms. “If I were to fall into the tender mercies of [targetFactionCorp], I doubt many commoners would willingly take my place. But enemies without are no less dangerous than enemies within. Girls looking to move up in [favoredFactionCorp] would gladly see me deposed if it meant they could climb a single rung higher. My superiors likewise are ever keen to keep those under them from plotting their own displacement. And that’s to say nothing of how essential it is to keep the respect of others at all times. A slight mistake could end up costing you the esteem of your friends and family. And if you look bad, they look bad.” She turns to you, with an earnest expression. “There’s a terran expression called ‘saving face,’ I believe. I’ve known women who sent their daughters down to the Gyre for good after one too many slips. And others who’ve been... ‘re-assigned’ to the XO corps, where following orders is no longer something they can mess up.” “In a sense, there’s far more freedom on the streets than in the penthouses,” she observes, her knife-like ears dipping lower. “They are employed by us, certainly, but they’re free to work for whichever Zaibatsu they choose. To leave and join up with another, if needs be. To make friends with anyone they choose and make whatever impulsive, rash actions they like.”"])))

You nod and stretch out in the bubbling pool. “Yeah, being a big wig can be tough. That’s why it’s neat that I get to be irresponsible on my quest-thingy and don’t have to worry about board meetings n’ stuff till later on!”
You admit that you hadn’t really considered the threats that the Peers have to worry about. Sounds like a perpetual tightrope walk.
“That’s probably why all of you have these XOs about, huh? Keep you safe from one another?”
“Just because there are risks and responsibilities doesn’t make living in the lap of luxury any less sweet,” you counter. “Still, glad to see the planet’s not only dangerous to the poor.”

Your hostess runs a finger along the seams of her cybernetics thoughtfully, but the warmth of the pool against her over-filled breasts pulls her out of the introspection quickly enough. She’s back to a flushed expression and snuggling her thick thighs against yours under the water in no time.

textify(LM||(LM=(0,Bt.Z)(["“You’d like to know a little bit about us?” the fancy lady inquires with a laugh. “Well, I’m happy to elaborate, but please forgive me if I come off as a little biased!”\n\n\t“So what do you do on Dhaal?”"])))

"+textify(jM||(jM=(0,Bt.Z)(["“Well, Paragon is primarily concerned with cybernetics. I guess you could say we’re in the hospital business: repairing, replacing, and improving anything that needs it. Of course, there’s plenty of augments that are purely cosmetic, as well.” Xhanni touches her own seams with pride. “If you live on Dhaal long enough, you’re likely to get a mod sooner or later. Some make life easier, some make it sweeter, but they all make Paragon richer. While Dhaal may be somewhat backwards when it comes to your galactic technology, we boast cybernetic advancements that eclipse even the sort of tech most of your worlds have access to.” She waves dismissively. “Of course, bio-tech is just the most interesting part of our portfolio. We have weapon fabrication plants and girls always working on the next, newest armament. Others are working on organic food growth systems, which is why we’ve been in talks with Carver Catering Company. Their expertise in food service could lift Paragon over even Bright Water, with their stranglehold on synthetic agriculture.” The Peer’s lips curl into a devious smile and she leans in close, her voice lowered to a whisper. “Actually, we’ve been making overt moves against our main rival, Utopian Skies, recently. The Capsule hotel by the space port, the Playground brothel... we’ve even found someone who can hack into their proprietary MindStream network for a Paragon alternative. Their CEO’s been acting off recently, so now’s the time to push them out and take over their holdings.” You rub your chin. “So you think Paragon would be a good company to control all of Dhaal?” “Oh, undoubtedly,” Anni replies. “Our CEO, Fabricator Marshal Rizzon Zau? She’s the youngest zaika to ever rise to Zaibatsu head. She’s got her finger on the pulse of the city and she’s not afraid of joining the galactic community now that this ‘Planet Rush’ has revealed the existence of intelligent alien life. With Paragon on top, who knows where we’ll go? We might find ways to spread the city comfortably into the dark side of the planet!”"])))

"+textify(RM||(RM=(0,Bt.Z)(["“Bright Water is a chemical concern,” Anni starts, her tail waving with enthusiasm. “You may have noticed that we kind of paved over the livable space on Dhaal. That means there’s not really very much room for agriculture or animal husbandry. We provide the essentials of life with synthetic food production. A few chemical tweaks here and there and we can generate all manner of dishes to keep the ballooning population well-fed.” You can recall seeing more than a few citizens in the Gyre looking far less than ‘well-fed,’ but you keep your silence while she goes on. “We also handle pharmaceuticals of every stripe: from medicine to liquor, to... recreational potables. You may have stopped by Aravok, the new milk bar near the space port? That’s us. Actually, the immigration of New Texans has proved a tremendous boon for Bright Water. The peculiar effects breast milk has on zaika has been as fascinating as enjoyable, and it’s been simply a delight to see all these off-world aliens rushing in to fill the planetary thirst for their delicious cream.” You can tell that even just the thought of milk is having an arousing effect on the Peer, whose bust size is slowly growing in the thermal pool. The Peer’s lips curl into a devious smile and she leans in close, her voice lowered to a whisper. “Actually, we have been noticing that with milk sales on the rise, there are fewer and fewer citizens turning to Utopian Skies’ MindStream for their primary entertainment. And that’s to say nothing of the impact on Paragon properties. They rely on weapons and augments, but If we can get girls out of the gang warfare on the streets and into our Milk Bars, where blissful tolerance reigns? We’ll be one step closer to ridding ourselves of those warmongers and their wretched guns.” You rub your chin. “So you think Bright Water would be a good company to control all of Dhaal?” “Oh, completely!” she gasps, nearly knocking the pink shades from her nose. “You met with Maven Jaqui Fassbin; surely you must’ve noticed her brilliance. She’s a one-in-a-generation mind. I’ve only had the honor to see her work a few times, but once inspiration hits her, she’s an artist! She even breaks into poetry, I hear. With someone like her at the helm, we’d be caught up to Galactic Standard tech in a week. We’d surpass it in a month. Representatives from XenoGen have been in talks with us already. Just think of everything Bright Water could offer, when science is guiding the ship!”"])))

"+textify(DM||(DM=(0,Bt.Z)(["“It’s said that Paragon guns might keep you safe and Bright Water food might keep you full, but Utopian Skies entertainment is what makes life worth living,” Anni recites, proudly. “The toil of life must have its reward, and that’s what we set out to offer. With our MindStream, people can escape the drudgery of life and let their imaginations reshape reality. They can speak with, play with, even lay with complete strangers on the other side of the planet instantly, adopting whatever roles in whatever scenario they choose. And this is no simple ‘VR program,’ like you may be familiar with. Every active element in the MindStream is piloted by an actress. Nothing is AI scripted. Every moan of pleasure comes from a real zaika connected to the stream elsewhere in the world!” The Dame tweaks her glasses and bounces her fingertips across the rounded islands her breasts make in the heated pool. “And that’s simply the most exciting part of what we do. Utopian Skies offers affordable housing with Capsule hotels, intimate gathering spaces with micro-bars, and employs more non-zaika immigrants than either other Zaibatsu with their brothels.” The Peer’s lips curl into a frown and she leans in close, her voice lowered to a whisper. “Admittedly, the Planet Rush has been difficult for us. Paragon has been using this opportunity to push into our territories, opening their own capsule hotels near the space port, and even now they’ve got a brand new brothel, taking visitors away from our facilities.” She lowers her voice even further. “But never fear: we’ve been importing ships of secret, exotic aliens for a total rehaul. You know about ‘galotians,’ I trust? I don’t know the particulars, but they’re going to solve all our problems with Paragon and give us the tools we need to unseat Bright Water once and for all.” You rub your chin. “So you think Utopian Skies would be a good company to control all of Dhaal?” “Without a doubt.” Xhanni nods vigorously. “We’ve been studying everything on your extranet and found that pleasure is the name of the game.” She pushes her glasses back up the bridge of her nose and waggles a finger. “Nobody does bliss like Utopian Skies, and with Executress Corellah Dova in charge, Dhaal will become the pleasure planet every alien wants to visit. You should see her work, these days: it’s like she’s a totally different person, with how vigorous she’s become. Maybe it’s the pressure from Paragon’s moves, but it seems like there’s nothing she can’t do. Supposedly, a representative from Tamani Corp has been in talks with her, and if Tamani herself comes? There’d be no stopping Utopian Skies!”"])))

textify(FM||(FM=(0,Bt.Z)(["You look over at Hollow and raise an eyebrow. “So, what’s the deal with the XOs?” Dame Xhanni tilts her head in confusion, then laughs. “Oh, excuse me. You probably didn’t see them all that often in the Gyre, did you? They’re absolutely everywhere in the Meridian. From what I understand of the galactic society at large, a ‘police’ force is fairly standard for governments to employ, so I suppose you could think of them like that, though it’s not quite that simple.” She raises a leg up, out of the water and wiggles her two big toes. As if commanded, the cyborg in the room moves around the edge of the pool and leans over to take hold of her Mistress’ foot in her organic hand, delicately kneading alien sole, from heel to tootsies and every muscle in between. Anni closes her eyes and leans back in supine rapture at the massage, her breath growing husky. “On Dhaal, we don’t have a government to speak of. People are free to do whatever they like. We - the Peers - do have to protect our goods and property, however, so all three Zaibatsu collaborated on the XO initiative quite some time ago. Possibly the last truly cooperative project we’ve done.” She lifts her other foot and Hollow changes her attention. “So they’re guards? Soldiers?” “Not in the sense you mean, I suspect.” The Peer blinks her eyes open and lowers her feet back into the pool while adjusting her glasses. “We don’t use them to enforce oppressive laws or anything. If an XO is dispatched to the Gyre, it’s to protect corporate interests from anarchists who’d rather live in caves than in civilization. And corporate warfare is typically done through our lesser employees. It doesn’t make sense to have XOs fight each other, no matter what color sash they might wear. If they’re captured, they can just be reprogrammed to obey the winning side.” “What are they, exactly?” “Cyborgs, and damned good ones at that. Absolute top of the line. Outfitted with sensors I certainly can’t explain, and combat protocols I’d never want to see. Paragon provided cybernetic frameworks for the metal half, Bright Water assisted with chemical augmentations to the organic half, and Utopian Skies makes sure the body is obedient while the mind inside is blissful. And all in the interest of keeping things safe and sound. A marvel of zaika engineering.” “But who WERE they?” you push. Anni sighs, the script falling away. “Prisoners, essentially,” she admits. “We don’t have jails in the way other races do. If someone proves unable to keep their freedom on Dhaal, they’re subdued and assigned a profession. For Paragon, that means tech-thralls. For Bright Water, it’s usually a test subject. For Utopian Skies, MindStream actress. These reassignments may be for a week, a month, a year, or longer; but the girls who go in eventually come back out, much improved, physically, mentally, and financially. In other words, better off than before. It’s a kind of corporate rehabilitation.” She turns to Hollow “But for those too treacherous to risk releasing back into civilian life, they are rebuilt from the ground up to be a part of the XO corps. They lose their names, their past is erased, and they’re given purpose: protection. It’s reserved for the most profoundly dangerous, but... there are always rumors that a certain deposed Peer was discretely transferred to XO duty when they prove too cruel even for the upper crust to tolerate.” “So why give yours a name?” The Dame waves and her XO leans way down for her owner to run her finger in loops over her gold-painted lips. She wears an inscrutable little smile, but doesn’t respond at first."])))

textify(PM||(PM=(0,Bt.Z)(["You decide to push the subject and try to find out more about the zaika lady’s personal bodyguard. Xhanni sighs and scoots away from you a few inches, turning to give you her full attention. “Am I truly so transparent? Yes... I knew Hollow before she went into the program. It’s hard to believe that the Peers would do... THAT to their own sort. “Well, there’s no real limit to what people might do, when power is on the line,” your hostess muses, crossing her legs. “Before you start making assumptions, let me assure you: it was not a product of my hand that made her this way. Indeed, she was beloved by those beneath her. Which is precisely why she was a threat to the woman who stood above her in our mad little political maze. An adored underling is just as dangerous as a scheming one.” She raises a conspiratorial eyebrow. “Our mutual superior removed her from the equation, to protect herself. But in doing so, she created a vacuum. One that I filled, schemes and all. Once she was out of the picture, I used my new position to try to discover what had become of... well, as you can see, I was too late to stop the conversion.” It’s hard to believe she’d take on somebody she obviously cared for as a roboticized slave! “Well...” Anni replies in a playful tone. “They may roll off the assembly line in a certain condition. She strides over to the poolside and bends down low to plant a little kiss on her owner’s cheek. “Wait,” you interject, confused. “I thought you said all that stuff got erased? And why call her by a pet name, when you could use her real one? And what even IS her real name?” The surprisingly emotive XO titters, holding a hand up to her mouth. “Well, maybe ‘erased’ wasn’t exactly the right word. My name was Konno; it’s very nice to make your acquaintance, [pc.name]. Our minds are locked away in here, oblivious to the world around us,” she explains, tapping her chromed helmet. “It’s not easy - or even very safe - to try to turn all that stuff off. And for some, it’s impossible. Even now, there could be a part of me still locked away in here somewhere, but I’d never know.” She shrugs. Xhanni winks and taps her lips. “As for the pet name... that’s for security. It wouldn’t do for someone to find out an XO had been tampered with. Making customizations is perfectly normal, but breaking the programming? Well, it’d be bad news for both of us. She does, however, go back to standing at attention, her face neutral and emotionless.
